heroText: Kampala is a capital city in Uganda, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Kampala
highlights.text: Kampala is a capital city in Uganda, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.label: Origins
history.text: Kampala originally referred to only the present-day Old Kampala hill, on whose summit Fort Lugard was located, and the initial headquarters of the British colonial authorities in the soon to be Uganda Protectorate.
In 1862, when explorer John Speke arrived in Buganda, the Kibuga (capital) was at Bandabarogo, present-day Banda Hill, and the reigning Kabaka (King) was Mutesa I.

history.label: 1870s
history.text: He also described the Kibuga in his 1870s dispatches to The New York Herald, thus: As we approached the capital, the highway from Usavara increased in width from 20 ft to 150 ft ...Arrived.
In 1875, explorer Henry Morton Stanley reported the capital as being at present-day Lubaga Hill, where he met the same Kabaka, Mutesa I.

In 1895, Mengo Senior School, the first school offering Western education in Kampala, was opened by the Church Missionary Society at Namirembe hill, where mostly the children of chiefs and pages of the royal.
Also in 1897, Kampala's first Western-style health facility, Mengo Hospital, was opened on Namirembe hill by British doctor and missionary Sir Albert Ruskin Cook.
This state of affairs later culminated in the signing of the Buganda Agreement (1900) that formalised British colonial rule in Buganda.

In 1906, the boundary of Kampala was set as a three-mile radius from the “present Nakasero Fort” at Old Kampala, and the Kampala Local Sanitary Board was designated as the authority for urban administration.

In 1922, Kampala's oldest university, Makerere, was founded as the Uganda Technical College at the present Makerere Hill and initially offered carpentry, building construction, mechanics, arts, education, agriculture, and medicine.
In 1931, the Uganda Railway line reached Kampala, connecting Kampala to Mombasa Port, thirty-five years after the commencement of its construction.
On 9 October, 1962, Uganda gained independence; subsequently the capital city was transferred from Entebbe to Kampala and in the same year, Kampala was granted city status.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Hence the name "Kampala" came to refer to this initial British colonial settlement that would later on spread out from the occupied Old Kampala hill near the pre-existing Kibuga (capital) of the Buganda Kingdom.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Captain Lugard would, later on, be allocated the Kampala hill that would soon be known as Old Kampala, and on which he built a fort.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The land in Buganda's Kibuga (capital), including Mengo Hill and Makerere Hill, was allocated to the young Kabaka, the Baganda colonial collaborators, etc., under mailo and freehold.
